title: Incorrect Max Heap Height Leading to Wrong Results

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, build a max heap such that the height of the max heap is always minimized. The height of a max heap of size n is the smallest integer h such that 2^(h-1) <= n <= 2^h - 1. You need to return the maximum value in the max heap.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers, where each integer represents a node in the max heap.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum value in the max heap.
statement.notes: The input array can be empty, in which case the max heap will also be empty.

examples.explanation: Max heap height is 3 in this case: [5, 9, 4, 8, 3, 2, 1]

hints.text: To build a max heap of minimized height, we need to ensure that the number of nodes in each sub-tree is as close to the power of 2 as possible.

editorial.title: Editorial: Minimizing Max Heap Height
editorial.intuition: Building a max heap of minimized height involves using a balanced binary heap.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would involve first finding the maximum value, then creating a max heap from the rest of the array.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a priority queue with a custom comparator to build a max heap and always keep the maximum value at the root.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a min-heap to store the indices of the elements in the array, then for each element, push it onto the min-heap and pop the root (which is the index of the maximum element in the array).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof involves demonstrating that the max heap constructed is always valid and the maximum value is correctly identified.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run demonstrates the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Not using a min-heap to store indices
Not handling the case when the max heap is empty
